desc: RefractV2 corner-lerp displacement model validated against headless t=0 flow. The exact-RNG field records + bilinear-phase-ramp (corner-lerp) displacement + a Size-independent Refraction gain reproduce FCP's measured flow. Confirms the decoded packing structure (NOT a plane-wave sum).
model: per octave A: c00=phA+axA*scale+ayA*px; c10=phA+axA*py+ayA*px; c01=phA+axA*scale+ayA*mm; c11=phA+axA*py+ayA*mm; argA(U,V)=bilerp(c00,c10,c01,c11;U,V)*2pi; offX+=sin(argA)*axA; offY+=sin(argA)*ayA; disp=gain*(offX,offY). U=x/W, V=y/H (approx; exact uses posx/sizeX+0.5 in the M-normalized frame).
findings: The Refraction gain (~47) is Size-INDEPENDENT (47.22 vs 47.08) -> confirms model generalizes.
Fitted 'scale' halves when Size doubles (0.21->0.11) -> scale ~ 1/Size (larger Size = lower spatial freq).
Ripple std halves when Size doubles (1.7->0.85) -> matches shader 1/sizeX coordinate scaling.
Spatial correlation 0.88-0.97 (not just DC) confirms the corner-lerp bilinear-phase-ramp form, disproving the plane-wave surrogate.

REMAINING: pin scale/px/py/mm to the exact M-matrix formulas (scale=Size*0.1 from const@0x268c50; px=M[0x460]/scale, py=M[0x438]/scale, mm=fneg) and the exact U=posx/sizeX+0.5 pixel mapping for a boot-free bit-exact port.

end_to_end.desc: node-boundary PSNR vs headless FCP at t=0 with FCT_UNDERWATER_EXACT (apply routes to underwaterApply): the wired exact path (exact-RNG field + corner-lerp displacement + inverse-map sign fix).
end_to_end.sign_fix: fieldOffsetExact disp matches FORWARD flow; the inverse-map resample negates it (fx=x-ox*g, fy=y-oy*g) -> +12 dB.
end_to_end.residual_to_40dB: validation-fitted kU/kV scalars (not yet exact M-projected) + bilinear resample; structure verified (corr 0.905-0.970).

residual_diagnosis.resample_order_test: bicubic (26.18) does NOT beat bilinear (26.58) -> residual is NOT resample-order-bound
residual_diagnosis.residual_structure: 80-90% of the 0.9px flow residual is explained by a QUADRATIC-in-(X,Y) term
residual_diagnosis.hypothesis: The RefractV2 shader has CROSS-FREQUENCY terms (lines 48-69: one octave's sin() feeds another octave's amplitude accumulation via P23-P28), producing products of octave sines = a quadratic spatial residual. The current model treats octaves independently. Decoding the exact P19-P28 amplitude cross-term wiring is the next refinement toward the 40 dB gate.
residual_diagnosis.status: displacement STRUCTURE + endpoint packing verified (corr 0.905-0.970, +12 dB e2e); cross-term amplitude wiring is the last refinement.

CORRECTION.cross_term_hypothesis: WRONG - disproven by direct shader trace.
CORRECTION.verified: HgcUnderwaterRefractV2 has exactly 10 sin() (8 scalar + 1 vec2 at line 66), one per octave, each weighted by its own amplitude slot P[19+i].x->offX / .y->offY and summed. NO cross-frequency products. The amplitude slots (loop @0x66300 reads record[-4],[0] = ampX,ampY = w*sin(ang),w*cos(ang)) = exactly the ampVec the model already uses.
CORRECTION.true_remaining_gap: the model is STRUCTURALLY EXACT (10 weighted sines, correct amp weights, corner-lerp endpoints). The 0.9px quadratic residual is the U,V mapping: I fit ONE global (kU0,kU1,kV0,kV1) + linear U=X/W, but the shader's U=posx/sizeX+0.5 uses posx from the PROJECTIVE M transform (r0.xy*=1/dot(tc,P2)). Pinning U,V to the exact host M (and its perspective divide) for the 1920x1080 canvas is the final step; the perspective test alone gave 0.902->0.862 so the full M likely closes most of the rest.

residual_diagnosis.hypothesis_CORRECTED: CORRECTION: re-traced the shader accumulation — it is NOT cross-frequency terms. There are exactly 10 sin() calls (8 scalar + 1 vec2 at line 66 = 10), one per octave, each weighted by its own P[19+i] amplitude vector into (offX,offY). The amplitude slots P19-28 (loop @0x66300) read (ampX,ampY)=(w*sin(ang), w*cos(ang)) — exactly the ampVec the model already uses. So the model STRUCTURE (10 weighted sines, correct amp weights) is COMPLETE and correct. The residual quadratic-in-(X,Y) term therefore comes from the BILERP ENDPOINT values (P9-18) / the U,V coordinate mapping not being exactly my 4 fitted linear constants — i.e. the exact host pixel-transform M projection of the endpoints (which makes U,V and the endpoints vary with a small projective/2nd-order dependence). Pinning M for the 1920x1080 canvas is the last step.

projective_uv_finding.structural: Adding a PROJECTIVE (homography) U,V mapping U=(m0*Xc+m1*Yc)/(m6*Xc+m7*Yc+1)+0.5 (V likewise) drops the Size=50 flow-fit residual 0.902->0.355px (near the ~0.5px centroid floor) and CROSS-VALIDATES at Size=100 (rms 0.84, corr 0.962/0.964 with the SAME homography+gain). This CONFIRMS the missing term is the shader's perspective divide (pos = M*texCoord * 1/dot(tc,P2)), not cross-frequency terms.
projective_uv_finding.overfit_caution: The 11-param homography FIT to Size=50 alone helps s50 end-to-end (29.6->32.6 dB) but REGRESSES s100 (30.2->28.0). Net ~neutral. Per decode-don't-fit, NOT shipped: a single-Size fit overfits. Kept the robust cross-validated LINEAR scalars (29.6/30.2 dB both Sizes).
projective_uv_finding.true_remaining_step: Pin the homography to the EXACT host pixel-transform M (stored at sp+0x438..0x468 as doubles in canThrowRenderOutput; P0..P2 forward + P3..P5 inverse) for the 1920x1080 canvas -- READ from the binary/runtime, not fitted -- so both Sizes improve together. That is the last step to the 40 dB gate.

homography_investigation.finding: A full projective U,V homography (11 params) drops the Size=50 flow residual 0.902->0.355px (near the ~0.5px centroid-measurement floor), CONFIRMING the missing term is the shader's PROJECTIVE M mapping (U=posx/sizeX+0.5 with posx from the perspective divide r0.xy*=1/dot(tc,P2)), NOT cross-terms.
homography_investigation.cross_val_flow: same homography+gain, kU/kV halved for Size=100: flow rms 0.842px corr 0.962/0.964 (generalizes in FLOW space).
homography_investigation.end_to_end: but wired end-to-end the 1-case-fit homography helps Size=50 (29.6->32.6 dB) and HURTS Size=100 (30.2->28.0 dB) = net-neutral overfit.
homography_investigation.decision: REVERTED per decode-don't-fit: kept the robust cross-validated LINEAR scalars (29.6/30.2 dB both Sizes). A ROBUST improvement needs the EXACT host pixel-transform M (read from the runtime FxImage), not an 11-param single-Size fit. The structural conclusion stands: projective U,V is the last gap to the 40 dB gate.
homography_investigation.verdict: model structurally exact (10 weighted sines, corner-lerp endpoints, projective U,V confirmed); shipped exact path = robust linear approx at ~30 dB; exact-M pin remains the final step.
